danger_signals.meaning: direct threat to player; single hit causes loss of life unless ship has barrier protection

danger_signals.visual_form: collision with enemy aircraft or ground units
danger_signals.meaning: immediate danger; results in arm loss or death depending on ship integrity

danger_signals.visual_form: visible removal of ship's arms/weapon appendages
danger_signals.meaning: degraded combat capability; loss of bomb-dropping ability; increased vulnerability

danger_signals.visual_form: absence of ambulance sprite after second arm loss
danger_signals.meaning: permanent incapacitation; no repair available; critical game-over state imminent

safety_signals.visual_form: red bell power-up; visual shield overlay around ship
safety_signals.meaning: temporary invulnerability; absorbs additional damage; critical defensive state

safety_signals.visual_form: ambulance vehicle sprite descending into play area after first arm loss
safety_signals.meaning: safe recovery opportunity; player can navigate to it to restore arm functionality
bonus points only; safe to collect but no combat benefit
screen-clear effect; eliminates all on-screen threats; temporary safety surge

faction_indicators.color_identity: cartoon-styled anthropomorphic spacecraft (visual identity varies by character: TwinBee vs WinBee)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: clouds containing hidden bells; ambulance vehicles for player support

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: air enemies (shoot bullets) vs ground enemies (drop bombs or release power-ups)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: enemy aircraft patterns; ground-based defensive units

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ship displays both weapon arms; full bomb-dropping capability visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player can execute dual attacks (air and ground targets); cooperative dual-ship attacks available in two-player mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: one arm missing from ship sprite; asymmetrical appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: bomb-dropping disabled; ambulance appears on screen; player must navigate to ambulance for repair

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: both arms missing; ship appears armless/unarmed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: no bomb capability; no ambulance arrives second time; player movement only; high vulnerability state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: shield overlay or barrier visual effect around ship (red bell state)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player can absorb additional hits without arm loss; temporary invulnerability window

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ship displays enhanced weapon configuration (white bell effect)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: increased firepower; dual bullet stream replaces single fire

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ship may display visual speed indicator or increased animation frames (blue bell, up to 5 levels)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enhanced ship mobility; navigation capability increases; dodge potential improves

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: image copies of ship visible alongside primary vessel (green bell effect)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: multiple firepower sources; coordinated attack pattern; significantly increased offensive capability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: all on-screen enemies eliminated simultaneously; visual eruption or removal effect
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: temporary threat elimination; brief safety window; enemy respawn follows

environmental_storytelling.example: bells hidden in floating clouds (sky territory); ground enemies drop alternate power-ups. Visually communicates resource scarcity and risk-reward (must shoot clouds to reveal bells, exposing ship to fire)

ambulance sprite arrival after arm damage. Communicates game world acknowledges player vulnerability; repair services exist as in-world safety net; visual representation of game-world logic (medical response to injury)
two-player vertical or horizontal alignment enables cooperative attacks. Visual togetherness signals enhanced power state; communicates teamwork mechanic through spatial relationship
visible removal of ship appendages; loss of bomb functionality tied to physical appearance change. Visually maps damage to capability loss; clear cause-effect relationship

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: can fire air weapons and drop bombs; standard movement speed; participates in collaborative attacks in two-player mode
cannot drop bombs; can still fire air weapons; reduced offensive output; must seek ambulance sprite
cannot fire or drop bombs; movement only; vulnerable to all incoming fire; no repair option available
scrolls down screen in attack patterns; fires projectiles at player ship; visible threat indicator
stationary or defensive; drop bombs upward or release power-up items; indicate zone control
descends into play area after first arm loss; awaits player collision for repair; disappears if collected or round ends
initially inert; reveal bells only when shot; communicate hidden resource availability

notes: TwinBee uses visual damage degradation (arm loss) as core world-state communication mechanic. Power-up system (colored bells) directly maps to visual ship modifications and behavioral capability changes. The ambulance system is unique narrative element: failure to reach it communicates permanent incapacitation. Two-player cooperation mechanics are visually reinforced through synchronized positioning. No faction conflict narrative present; threat model is generic enemy forces vs player craft. Cute aesthetics mask harsh difficulty curve—power-up loss upon death creates visual/mechanical feedback loop. Environmental storytelling relies on cloud-concealment mechanic and medical-response system rather than narrative/political elements.
sources_used: wikipedia: TwinBee gameplay section, power-up system documentation, ambulance mechanics, damage system, cooperative attack mechanics
